title: Feedback Loops

content.statement: Systems run on feedback, not effort.
content.question: Which part of your life or business are you running without a feedback loop?
sop: Pick one area currently running without any feedback signal.
Add one simple feedback source: a weekly number, a client check-in, a self-check metric.
Rate your Current state and score incoming Feedback each week (see calculation, page 6).
Adjust your next action based on that feedback, not on how you feel about it.
Review weekly on a fixed schedule, don't wait for a crisis to check in.
questions: Which part of your life or business are you running without a feedback loop?
The last piece of feedback you got that stung a little, did you actually change course, or just note it and move on?
What decision are you about to make with zero real signal to check it against?

definitions.def: When a system's output is fed back as input, allowing self-correction or self-amplification.

content.equation_example: Current state rated 6 out of 10, Feedback a good result rated plus 2. Next Action equals 6 plus 2 = 8, so you push further in that direction. With a bad result, Feedback negative 2, Next Action equals 6 minus 2 = 4, so you pull back. Consistently applying feedback keeps the number tracking reality; ignoring feedback means your plan drifts away from what's actually happening.
content.closing_statement: No feedback means no way to actually correct course.

caption:
Principle Nº 120: Feedback Loops

Systems run on feedback, not effort.

Ask yourself: Which part of your life or business are you running without a feedback loop?
